content:
HI @Hixie.

I come across two things. 
1. I need a iOS style scan text icon, but I can't find it in `CupertinoIcons`, would you mind telling me how to get that or how to add it to framework? ^_^
2. Is there a way to regenerate the `arb` file? I want add a new localiztion text string `scanTextButtonLabel`. Or I should manually add it for all different languages ?
O(∩_∩)O
Hi @justinmc. Would you mind sharing your thoughts on current implementation? O(∩_∩)O
Hi @luckysmg, I'm eagerly looking forward to the merge of this feature! What's the status of this PR?
Sorry @luckysmg I missed your review request.  Can you fix the merge conflicts here?
Sure. I ll do that later.

It looks like the customer tests are failing because there is a breaking change from adding startLiveTextInput to TextInputClient.  Is there any reasonable way around making a breaking change there?  I mentioned splitting this out of TextInputClient before, but maybe that's not practical.

If not, we'll need to follow the steps in this [breaking change guide](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Tree-hygiene#handling-breaking-changes).

> Is there any reasonable way around making a breaking change there? 

I will have a try on that. ^_^
@luckysmg How's it going with this, anything else I can help with?  Looks like you have a conflict and need to push a merge commit.
I m working on some busy work. Will continue when I have time. ^_^
Sounds good, keep us posted.

> Sounds good, keep us posted.

Yes. I will mark this PR ready for review when it is ready ^_^
@justinmc I think it is ready for re-review ^_^

> Should this work on a simulator? I know it needs a camera, but when I try it, nothing happens. I don't know if that's expected or not. I think this is expected. The ios native UITextField has same behavior on simulator.

All nits Done.  And I add color for judging dark mode.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/49340347/53662c40-a66f-49e7-86f7-fab6732c1e5d
Done.
@tgucio Would you mind re-review? Since you have a change request that block this PR merging  ^_^
@luckysmg Added a few comments: mostly nits and localisation remarks. Again thanks for this wonderful work!
@tgucio  All Done. plz re-review ^_^
@luckysmg I've LGTM'd with nits: remaining full stop in Urdu l10n and some doubles without decimal point in `text_selection_toolbar_button.dart` - could you look into those before merging?

Yes. I ll take a look before merging...  Will change. ^_^

Before I didn't understand your meaning very well haha ^_^
@tgucio I think all nits done. You can take a look
Also @justinmc would you mind help to take a look that why google testing still not completed? ^_^
Google tests look fine despite the stuck "pending" status.  I tried to change the status but it is giving an HTTP error. Merging.
@luckysmg Thanks for all your work here!
@luckysmg gj :)
Congrats to @luckysmg and @tgucio for the shout out in the 3.13 blog post! https://medium.com/flutter/whats-new-in-flutter-3-13-479d9b11df4d

## Slider

## Description

Update the Slider and RangeSlider to the latest Material spec. This introduces an updated track, updated thumbs, updated tick marks, and a new value indicator shape. The old paddle value indicator shape is also updated so that text scaling works consistently.

Full details of this change can be found here: https://groups.google.com/forum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer#!msg/flutter-announce/69dmlKUL5Ew/tQh-ajiEAAAJ

## Tests

I added the following tests:

Updated all affected tests in slider_test, range_slider_test, and slider_theme_test
Also kept the original paddle value indicator tests and added a new test for the new default rectangular shape.

Exactly same error here, tried the same things
Did you update your platform tools to 29 as well?

I updated everything yesterday, there was 3 or 4 tools updates to do, i think platform tools was one of them.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7141084/58988842-eeb3be00-87b8-11e9-8b06-8e5ab65e7079.png)

I was working on a project in Android studio stable. But auto imports are only supported in the Android studio beta. I downloaded the Beta version on my windows. and boom everything was broken. The installation updated the platform sdk to 29

I'm setting up everything to work on Windows, installed Android Studio using JetBrains Toolbox, installed all de SDKs and tools, but when i tried to build on emulator it showed up the error, moved to real device, same thing, then i started looking for solutions

On stable i'm getting the same error after updating to 29. You can do a quick test to see the error using flutter doctor instead of running your project. Just make sure your device is running / connected.

I found a temporary fix while we wait for a solution provided by the Flutter team

Windows: http://dl-ssl.google.com/android/repository/platform-tools_r28.0.2-windows.zip
macOS: http://dl-ssl.google.com/android/repository/platform-tools_r28.0.2-darwin.zip
Linux: http://dl-ssl.google.com/android/repository/platform-tools_r28.0.2-linux.zip

this download contains the platform tools for 28.0.1

if you back up your platform-tools folder and place the content of the zip in the platform-tools folder. 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21172855/58989489-4f0c2500-87e4-11e9-8b72-6a9b8ebf0e5f.png)

this should be in your platform-tools folder
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21172855/58989511-59c6ba00-87e4-11e9-8e9d-e2d055416863.png)

**THIS IS A TEMPORARY FIX, NOT A SOLUTION**
I prefer going back to my Linux boot where i haven't upgraded it, but thats a nice workaround, thanks for it!

Aaah nice. I couldn't find the links :D thanks. I updated my comment as well

@brunocerk Downgrading isn't hard.
1. Navigate to the Android SDK folder.
2. Remove `platform-tools` folder.
3. Unzip downloaded `platform-tools` from version 28.0.2, the last version before 29.0.0.

On Bash these are the commands you need (run at Android SDK home):
```bash
rm -r platform-tools
wget -O platform-tools.zip http://dl-ssl.google.com/android/repository/platform-tools_r28.0.2-linux.zip
unzip platform-tools.zip
rm platform-tools.zip
```

@vanlooverenkoen Thanks for this temporary fix.

For anyone who does not know where to find the `platform-tools` folder (at least I did not know :wink:), the folder screenshot in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/33938#issuecomment-499250288 can be found at the following location:

`C:\Users\[your-username]\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk`

(`AppData` is a hidden folder by default)
Same problem, fix platform-tools
Its impressive how 2 google projects dont take care of this massive issues in consideration. This workaround works for now...
